jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer driving, likely crashing or getting into an accident during opening credits
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer 'D'oh!' followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase is perfectly integrated to his character and economical, but it's predictable opening credits material

jokes.text: Let's welcome two new additions to the team... Smash Diggins and Fishbone Walker. Lucky for us, they were sent down from the majors... for drug violations.
jokes.setup: Announcer introducing new players as if they're positive additions
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were demoted for drug violations, making them 'lucky for us' ironically
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure with ironic twist, but the drug violation joke is somewhat predictable sports humor

jokes.text: Why do you hate the Isotopes so much, Dad? Because I loved them once and they broke my heart. Let that be a lesson to you, sweetie. Never love anything. Even you? Especially me.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his cynicism about the team
jokes.punchline: Escalates from 'never love anything' to 'especially me' when Lisa asks about loving her
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation that's perfectly in character for Homer's selfish nihilism, with great economy and structure

jokes.text: That's right. Like my mother always said... you've gotta stick it out, even if you picked a loser. Hmm. To the bitter end.
jokes.setup: Marge giving advice about supporting the team even if they're losers
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'Hmm' reaction suggests he's applying this to their marriage
jokes.explanation: Subtle joke that works through Homer's reaction beat, implying marriage parallel with excellent economy

jokes.text: His career's over. I'm gonna warm up the car. But there's only been one pitch. And it sucked.
jokes.setup: Player gets injured on first pitch, Homer declares career over
jokes.punchline: Homer's ready to leave after one pitch because 'it sucked'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's impatience and negativity, but fairly standard sports fan humor

jokes.text: Of course, he's no Babe Ruth III... but the franchise is very excited about this illegitimate great grand bambino.
jokes.setup: Introducing Babe Ruth IV as a player
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that he's an 'illegitimate great grand bambino'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'bambino' and absurd family lineage concept, though not deeply character-specific

jokes.text: And what's this? He's pointing to the right field bleachers. Probably at a dying little boy.
jokes.setup: Classic baseball trope of player pointing to where he'll hit home run
jokes.punchline: Casually assumes he's pointing at a dying child instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of baseball cliche with unexpected dark twist, great economy and structure

jokes.text: Mom, am I dying? No, of course not. Is he, Mom? You can tell me. No!
jokes.setup: Bart overhears announcer's comment about dying boy
jokes.punchline: Bart persistently asks if he's dying despite Marge's denials
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke with child's innocent persistence creating discomfort

jokes.text: So who won? The losers? No, they lost. [Chuckles] Losers.
jokes.setup: Homer asking who won the game
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about 'losers' winning, then laughing at them being losers
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay showing Homer's limited thinking, adequate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You'll be in your cold, cold grave before that ever happens.
jokes.setup: Discussion about team maybe making playoffs
jokes.punchline: Homer's extremely dark prediction about when playoffs might happen
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation with memorable phrasing, though fairly standard pessimistic Homer

jokes.text: Homer, would you please stop talking about the children's graves?
jokes.setup: Homer's previous dark comment
jokes.punchline: Marge's exasperated response implies this is a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation suggesting Homer regularly talks about children's graves, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Did Moe finally blow his brains out?
jokes.setup: Homer hearing crowd excitement
jokes.punchline: Homer's first guess is that Moe committed suicide
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly fitting Homer's callousness and the show's irreverent tone

jokes.text: Yeah, that sniper at the all-star game was a blessing in disguise.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the team is suddenly good
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions sniper violence as positive thing
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark absurdist humor with casual treatment of violence as sports benefit

jokes.text: I want to thank Jesus and say hi to my special lady, Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer giving interview thanking Jesus
jokes.punchline: Treating wife like secondary acknowledgment after Jesus
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's priorities and casual treatment of Marge

jokes.text: They lost? Those losers! No, no, no. The game's not over. Whoo! Not over! Whoo!
jokes.setup: Homer calling team losers when he thinks they lost
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches back to cheering when corrected
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's fickle loyalty and bandwagon mentality

jokes.text: Ah, nobody touched my rumaki. [Screams] Rumaki!
jokes.setup: Homer noticing food wasn't eaten during celebration
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately grabs it, Homer screams about rumaki
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food obsession moment with funny word 'rumaki' and timing

jokes.text: Hey, who's on first? Touchdown!
jokes.setup: Classic Abbott and Costello reference at baseball celebration
jokes.punchline: Someone yells 'Touchdown!' mixing up sports
jokes.explanation: Simple drunk confusion humor, adequate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: What did you do last night? Last night? Um-
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer about his activities
jokes.punchline: Homer's hesitation and cut to flashback implies shenanigans
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for flashback sequence, functional but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Mm, uh, hello, big Maggie. Yes! Yes! Yes!
jokes.setup: Homer apparently seeing Maggie enlarged or distorted
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic reaction to whatever he's seeing
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that's hard to fully appreciate from transcript alone, seems like drunk vision humor

jokes.text: That's why we're jumping to the conclusion that this was the work of no-good punk kids.
jokes.setup: Chief saying they have no suspects
jokes.punchline: Admits they're 'jumping to conclusions' while presenting it as logic
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment showing his incompetent reasoning presented as official logic

jokes.text: Any kid caught on the street after dark will be shot... or returned to their parents, as the situation may warrant.
jokes.setup: Announcing curfew enforcement
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions shooting kids before backing down to reasonable alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation perfectly fitting Wiggum's character, great structure and economy

jokes.text: Serves you little punks right. Maybe next time you'll think before- Oh, my God! Look what those rotten kids did to my car!
jokes.setup: Homer lecturing about kids getting what they deserve
jokes.punchline: Immediately discovers his own car was vandalized
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal with Homer becoming victim right after supporting punishment

jokes.text: Sure you do. You have the right to remain silent. [Both Laughing] That was cold-blooded, Marge. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about rights
jokes.punchline: Homer twists Miranda rights into telling kids to shut up
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay using legal reference, good parent solidarity moment

jokes.text: Well, if kids are so innocent, why is everything bad named after them? Acting childish, kidnapping, child abuse. What about adultery? Not until you're older, Son.
jokes.setup: Homer arguing kids aren't innocent using word etymology
jokes.punchline: Bart mentions adultery, Homer says he can't learn about it yet
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay argument with good Bart comeback and Homer deflection

jokes.text: Achtung, babies! Curfew is in effect!
jokes.setup: Wiggum enforcing curfew with authority
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Achtung babies' mixing German command with cutesy term
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with authoritarian language mixed with baby talk, economical

jokes.text: Oh, uh, sorry to disturb you, Dr. Hibbert. [Deep Voice] Not at all, officer. [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Wiggum checking IDs during curfew
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert apparently in disguise but recognized, laughs inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Dr. Hibbert's signature laugh in awkward situation

jokes.text: When was the last time you played Citizenship? Energy Shortage? Hippo in the House? Ooh, the Game of Lent!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting old board games for entertainment
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and depressing board game titles
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist game names that satirize both boring educational games and depressing real-world issues

jokes.text: Fine. We'll play Hippo in the House. Oh, the hippo's missing. [Both Groaning]
jokes.setup: Kids resigned to playing the absurd board game
jokes.punchline: Can't even play it because the main piece is missing
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up frustration to previous joke, economical disappointment

jokes.text: She's got problems. Scary problems.
jokes.setup: Milhouse talking about having to talk to his mom all night
jokes.punchline: Ominous reference to mom's 'scary problems'
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable overshare from Milhouse about family dysfunction

jokes.text: Your metabolism will change someday too, young man.
jokes.setup: Kids mocking adults' big butts
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact response about metabolism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - practical, slightly defensive, totally matter-of-fact

jokes.text: Well, you gotta spend money to make money, Lou. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Lou commenting on expensive curfew enforcement equipment
jokes.punchline: Wiggum misapplies business cliche to police work
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment showing his confused understanding of business/police work

jokes.text: You robbed me of my manhood! That's petty theft. [TV Audience] Ooh! Don't go there.
jokes.setup: TV show dialogue about manhood being robbed
jokes.punchline: Response treats metaphorical 'robbery' as literal petty theft
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay joke in fake TV show, satirizing sitcom humor conventions

jokes.text: But even the new management of Sony TriStar... could not contain the pure evil of... The Bloodening.
jokes.setup: Horror movie trailer describing sealed evil film
jokes.punchline: Blames corporate ownership change for evil being unleashed
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate Hollywood and horror movie marketing tropes

jokes.text: Milhouse, do you see what's on Channel 6? Uh, yeah. It's really something. [Screams]
jokes.setup: Bart asking Milhouse about scary movie
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's understated response followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse character moment with understated fear followed by panic

jokes.text: Life insurance? Hmm. Will I be able to borrow against the equity? I don't know, sir. It comes free with the popcorn.
jokes.setup: Movie theater offering life insurance for scary movie
jokes.punchline: Skinner seriously considers financial aspects of free promotional insurance
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner character comedy showing his penny-pinching nature applied to absurd situation

jokes.text: Skinner! We're losing valuable make-out time. [Giggles]
jokes.setup: Skinner obsessing over insurance details
jokes.punchline: Edna reminds him they're there to make out, not analyze insurance
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable moment revealing Skinner-Edna relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Actually, we prefer to stay. You're thinking about hurting us. Now you're thinking... 'How did they know what I was thinking?' Now you're thinking, 'I hope that's shepherd's pie in my knickers.'
jokes.setup: Creepy movie children reading adult's mind
jokes.punchline: Mind reading escalates from threatening to embarrassingly specific
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from sinister to absurdly specific bodily concern, great structure

jokes.text: Man, I never liked Shakespeare until now.
jokes.setup: Watching violent horror movie with British accents
jokes.punchline: Bart mistakes horror movie for Shakespeare due to accents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart ignorance - equates British accents with high culture regardless of content

jokes.text: And you pilfered the poor box. And, Doctor, we know that you and the bootblack... have been rogering the fishwife in the crumpet shop.
jokes.setup: Movie children revealing townspeople's secrets
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurdly specific Victorian-era sexual scandal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation using archaic language for sexual scandal, very quotable and specific

jokes.text: Take that, you suckers! Stick him with the pitchfork! Way to go! In your face, fishwife!
jokes.setup: Kids cheering for movie violence against adults
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic support for pitchfork violence, callback to fishwife
jokes.explanation: Good callback and shows kids' bloodthirsty enthusiasm, but fairly straightforward

jokes.text: Listen up, punks. The moral of the story is... the adults always win. For crying out loud, Eddie. You scared the hell out of me.
jokes.setup: Wiggum delivering tough lesson to kids
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets scared by his own officer
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic undermining of Wiggum's authority right after declaring adult superiority

jokes.text: Don't forget to clean under the jowls. That spot is Club Med for mildew.
jokes.setup: Wiggum giving kids cleaning duties as punishment
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and gross metaphor about jowl hygiene
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific absurd metaphor, 'Club Med for mildew' is perfectly crafted and memorable

jokes.text: And let this be a lesson to you! Kids never learn! [Snickers]
jokes.setup: Wiggum telling kids to learn from punishment
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself saying kids never learn anyway
jokes.explanation: Good self-contradicting Wiggum moment showing his lack of logical thinking

jokes.text: Homer's done a ton of crap that never made the papers. My mom shoplifts all the time. Stuff she doesn't even need. My dad gets in car accidents on purpose.
jokes.setup: Kids planning to reveal adult secrets
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dark revelations about their parents' behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalating dark humor about Springfield parents' various criminal/dysfunctional behaviors

jokes.text: No! We have to reach people whose opinions actually matter. And I think I know how.
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting they put secrets on the Internet
jokes.punchline: Lisa dismissing Internet as reaching people whose opinions don't matter
jokes.explanation: Prescient joke about Internet's lack of influence in late 90s, good Lisa character moment

jokes.text: 200 channels, nothin' but cats.
jokes.setup: Homer channel surfing late at night
jokes.punchline: Finds only cat programming on 200 channels
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on cable TV proliferation, economical and memorable

jokes.text: Tom Shales says the writing 'snaps, crackles and pops.'
jokes.setup: Marge quoting TV critic about show quality
jokes.punchline: Uses cereal slogan to describe TV writing
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay satirizing lazy TV criticism, but fairly obvious Rice Krispies reference

jokes.text: Constable Wiggum likes to act tough... but he also likes to walk the beat in control-top panty hose.
jokes.setup: Kids revealing adults' embarrassing secrets
jokes.punchline: Specific humiliating detail about Wiggum's undergarments
jokes.explanation: Good embarrassing revelation that's specific and character-appropriate for Wiggum

jokes.text: Well, it's not like that's a crime. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Learning about Wiggum's cross-dressing
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance with nervous laugh
jokes.explanation: Good Homer reaction showing his awkward attempt at liberal acceptance

jokes.text: Did you know he likes to eat out of the Flanders' garbage? Oh, Homer. I have a problem.
jokes.setup: Kids revealing Homer's secret behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic admission that he has a problem
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment with pathetic admission and Marge's disappointed reaction

jokes.text: Well, at least they've already done me. And we have plenty more on Homer Simpson. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he's safe from more revelations
jokes.punchline: Immediately learns there's more dirt on him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with classic Homer false security followed by disappointment

jokes.text: Our top secret tonight- gay divorcee Luann Van Houten... has been cheating on her boyfriend, Pyro... with his best friend, Gyro.
jokes.setup: Kids revealing adult relationship drama
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous names 'Pyro' and 'Gyro' for boyfriends
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with matching food/fire names, absurd but memorable

jokes.text: I have captured the signal and am presently triangulating the vectors... and compressing the data down... in order to express it as a function of my hand. They're over there!
jokes.setup: Frink using complex scientific language to track kids
jokes.punchline: All his technical jargon just means pointing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Frink character comedy with perfect structure - overwrought science-speak for simple pointing

jokes.text: Brilliant. They transduced amplitude modulation... via the concavity of that oversized beverage conveyance. I mean, that is some clever 'viving.'
jokes.setup: Frink explaining kids' radio setup in technical terms
jokes.punchline: Switches to slang 'viving' to sound hip
jokes.explanation: Excellent Frink character moment trying to be cool with made-up slang after technical explanation

jokes.text: We promise we won't kill ya. Speak for yourself.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to reassure kids
jokes.punchline: Lou immediately contradicts with death threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation with perfect economy - Lou's deadpan threat undermines Wiggum's reassurance

jokes.text: This is Kent Brockman reporting from... my own home... in accordance with the new curfew for anyone under 70.
jokes.setup: News reporter doing remote broadcast
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's at home due to senior citizen curfew
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the conflict resulted in reverse curfew affecting adults

jokes.text: This is Kent Brockman reporting from... my own home... in accordance with the new curfew for anyone under 70.
jokes.setup: News report about new curfew developments
jokes.punchline: Curfew has been expanded to affect almost all adults, trapping reporter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how generational warfare has backfired on adults

jokes.text: You really should've voted, Homer. [Scoffs] It wouldn't have made a difference.
jokes.setup: Learning the curfew passed by one vote
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive attitude despite his vote being the deciding factor
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer character moment showing his civic irresponsibility with perfect ironic timing

jokes.text: Hey, fellas! Hey, you wanna stop with the kicking? My pills are in that can! Good gravy! I don't kick your things.
jokes.setup: Adults celebrating taking back the night
jokes.punchline: Elderly man being victimized by the adults who supposedly won
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal showing adults aren't much better than kids, but fairly standard

jokes.text: When you're trying to breathe on the machine, do I go up and kick it? Oh, now look at that. It went down the sewer! You happy? I'm gonna sue now! Now it's time for the lawyers!
jokes.setup: Old man complaining about his pills being kicked
jokes.punchline: Escalates to threatening lawsuits over kicked pill container
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but fairly predictable old man griping and lawsuit threats
